ingredients.name: Angle as Rotation
ingredients.description: An angle represents the amount of rotation from a reference direction.

ingredients.name: Point on the Unit Circle
ingredients.description: Every angle corresponds to a unique point on the unit circle.

ingredients.name: Sine as Y-Coordinate
ingredients.description: Sine of an angle equals the y-coordinate of the corresponding unit circle point.

bridges.description: Understanding opposite, adjacent, hypotenuse enables sine as a ratio.

geometric.title: Radius Creates Height
geometric.body: A rotating radius can be split into horizontal and vertical parts. The vertical part changes with the angle.
geometric.visual_hint: Draw a circle with a radius at angle theta, then drop a vertical line to the x-axis.

algebraic.title: The y-Part of a Circle Point
algebraic.body: A point on a circle of radius r has vertical coordinate r sin(theta).

card_templates.prompt: Which part of the radius controls height?
